title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Inspector Corradino travels by Daimler to an ancient mansion, the former home of the wealthy and disabled Mrs. Goddard. Upon his arrival, he is greeted by a butler and meets Nurse MacLane, who warns him that Mrs. Goddard is a dying woman kept alive primarily by morphine and a strong will. The nurse cautions that Mrs. Goddard may suddenly collapse into a semi-comatose state during their meeting.

Corradino is led to a large bedroom to interview the frail but spirited Bella Goddard. During their conversation, Mrs. Goddard discusses her late husband, Randall, and his connection to Blanche Blacklock. She explains that because their only son died at age two, Randall left his money to Blanche, whom he trusted for her upright character and judgment. Mrs. Goddard notes that Blanche had previously assisted Randall with financial difficulties. She also describes the upbringing of the Blacklock sisters, noting that Letitia is a responsible woman who returned home to care for her disabled sister after their tyrannical father died.

When questioned about other potential heirs, Mrs. Goddard reveals that Randall had a sister named Sonya, though they were estranged because Randall disapproved of her marriage to Dimetri Stanfordtis. Mrs. Goddard believes Dimetri was a genuine fraud with a criminal record, though she notes that Sonya loved him regardless. Despite her chronic illness and various losses, Mrs. Goddard expresses a sense of gratitude for the life she has lived.
